title: StampJumpRow — Tappable Stamp Chips for Waveform Seek
problem: Waveform players and editors display stamps (🔥⭐❓💡) at their recorded timestamps. These stamps are most useful when tappable — jumping to the timestamp lets the band quickly find the moments that were marked during recording.
analysis: StampJumpRow renders a horizontal row of tappable stamp chips. Each chip is a `<button>` element (not a div) with aria-label='Jump to M:SS'. On tap: calls `onSeek(stamp.timestampMs / 1000)` which is passed in from the parent (WaveformPlayer, WaveformEditor, PublicPlayer). Chip colors match stamp type (fire=red, keep=yellow, uncertain=orange, idea=blue). The row accepts an optional `className` prop for layout customisation across its three usage contexts.
successCriteria:
- Chips rendered as <button> elements (keyboard accessible, screen-reader compatible)
- Each chip: aria-label='Jump to M:SS' (time formatted in human-readable minutes:seconds)
- Tap calls onSeek with timestamp in seconds
- Chip colors: fire=#ef4444, keep=#eab308, uncertain=#f97316, idea=#3b82f6
- Hidden when stamps array is empty (returns null)
- className prop accepted for layout context (WaveformPlayer vs. Editor vs. PublicPlayer)
implementation: components/clips/StampJumpRow.tsx: props { stamps: Stamp[], onSeek: (timeSecs: number) => void, className?: string }. Maps stamps to button elements. formatTime(ms) → 'M:SS' helper. Returns null when stamps.length === 0.

decisions.decision: Stamp chips are <button> elements, not styled divs
decisions.rationale: Div elements are not keyboard-accessible and do not reliably receive touch events on all browsers. <button> is semantically correct for interactive elements and gets keyboard focus, Enter/Space activation, and proper touch event handling for free.
decisions.recommendation: Always <button>, never <div onClick>. aria-label provides screen-reader context.

knownLimitations: Stamp chips are ordered by creation (recording order), not by timestamp — if a user stamped out of order, chips appear out of sequence relative to the waveform
userGuide: Colored dots along the bottom of the waveform mark moments stamped during recording. Tap any dot to jump to that moment in the audio.
